rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE R. GENE GEISLER TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 28, 1992, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order: 1) Compelling Respondent Joseph Capko?S Compliance With Requests For Production, Set One, And 2) For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Part 1 of 2 This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Before the court are: (1) Petitioner Nancy Nowotny's Motion for Order Compelling Respondent Joseph Capko's Compliance with Request for Production, Set One; and for Monetary Sanctions; (2) Respondent Joseph Capko's opposition to the motion and request for sanctions; and, (3) Capko's evidentiary objections. Respondent Joseph Capko's evidentiary objections to paragraph 4 and Exhibit B are SUSTAINED; his objections to paragraph 5 and Exhibit C are OVERRULED. Nowotny has not carried her initial burden of showing that she engaged in reasonable and good faith attempts at informal resolution of the issues raised in her motion. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2031.301, subd. (b)(2); see Code Civ. Proc., section 2016.040.) Nowotny had the factual basis for her further demand as early as March 14, but delayed conferring for 43 days, then twice in quick succession demanded an extension of time for her motion to compel without disclosing the existing deadline and, ultimately, filed her motion to compel only three days after commencing discussions. Even if the court agreed that Nowotny did not have the factual basis as to the emails until April 7-and the court does not so agree, she still delayed conferring for another 18 days without justification and she has not shown her efforts to resolve the disputes were reasonable and in good faith. Nowotny's submission of the excerpt from the March 30 deposition transcript with her reply was untimely for purposes of carrying her initial burden; any way, the late evidentiary submission does demonstrate that Nowotny timely initiated discussions to informally resolve the dispute over the email or otherwise engaged in reasonable and good faith attempts at informal resolution of any of the issues raised in her motion. Separately, as demonstrated in the opposition and reply, Nowotny's request for an order compelling further production in response to Request for Production, Set One, Numbers 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, 17, 19, 22, 24, 28, 29, 30, 31, 38 is moot. Nowotny's demand for an amended privilege log fails because, viewed in context, the privilege log provides sufficient information to allow the court to determine whether the claimed privilege applies. In any event, disagreement regarding the privilege log likely could have been resolved, if Nowotny timely initiated discussions. Nowotny's demand for production of specific documents, such as Bates #003 and #013, is not supported by the record in that she has failed to establish which documents were not produced. For example, without evidentiary support, Nowotny asserts in her reply brief that it has not been produced, yet Capko had already provided testimony under penalty of perjury that Bates #003 was produced. If Nowotny wanted the court to find otherwise, she should have provided, at the very least, admissible evidence that could support a finding that Bates #003 was not produced. Nowotny's claim that the documents over which a privilege is asserted are, in fact, not privileged is not supported by this record. Nowotny's separate statement does not comply with California Rules of Court, rule 3.1345. Subsequent failures by Nowotny to comply with relevant statutes and rules will likely not be excused. Part 1 of 2

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE R. GENE GEISLER TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 28, 1992, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order: 1) Compelling Respondent Joseph Capko?S Compliance With Requests For Production, Set One, And 2) For Monetary Sanctions (Added For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Part 2 of 2 Nowotny's request for sanctions under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2031.310(h) and 2023.010 is DENIED. Subject to exceptions not relevant here, "the court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010 ) against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully  opposes a motion to compel further response to a demand, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." (Code Civ. Proc., secction 2031.310, subd. (h).) Here, Capko's opposition was successful, so sanctions are not authorized. Capko's request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The court assumes Capko's request is under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2031.310(h) and, in any event, sanctions are mandatory where, as here, a party unsuccessfully makes a motion to compel further response to a demand, unless the finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2031.310, subd. (h).) The record does not demonstrate that Nowotny acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. Sanctions are set at $2,800. Capko's request is denied to the extent it seeks sanction exceeding $2,800. Nowotny is to pay Capko $2,800 within 10 days of notice of entry of this order. Capko is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on Thursday, June 9, 2022 Remote appearance is encouraged. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
